preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the palatal glide [j]) into the lateral [l] is a form of liquidization, documented in contact linguistics and bilingualism where the phonetic spaces of glides and liquids overlap.

examples.note: Phonetic drift observed in language contact environments where glides are reinterpreted as liquids (e.g., in certain creolization or attrition processes).

sources: Phonological research on glide-liquid category overlap.
